original_abstract:
Imbalanced learning represents a significant hurdle in data mining and machine learning, as traditional algorithms frequently struggle to perform accurately when class distributions are highly skewed. This paper provides a comprehensive survey of recent breakthroughs and methodologies designed to mitigate the bias inherent in learning from imbalanced data. Unlike conventional reviews that focus almost exclusively on binary classification within traditional machine learning, this study broadens the scope to include contemporary challenges such as imbalanced regression, unsupervised clustering, and the complexities of deep long-tailed learning. 

We categorize existing solutions into five primary domains: data-level preprocessing, algorithm-level modifications, hybrid strategies, ensemble learning frameworks, and techniques for handling non-stationary data streams. Furthermore, the paper provides a detailed taxonomy of real-world applications—ranging from bioinformatics and cybersecurity to industrial fault diagnosis and business management—illustrating how these methods are deployed in practice. By synthesizing the current state of the art, we identify six emerging research challenges and outline future directions, such as multimodal imbalanced learning and federated learning under skew, aimed at guiding the next generation of researchers in developing more robust and equitable intelligent systems.
humanized_abstract:
Learning from imbalanced data is one of the most serious issues encountered in the field of data mining and machine learning. Most learning algorithms fail to produce accurate classification models for highly imbalanced datasets. This survey paper tries to summarize the recent progress in this field by giving some effective methods that can reduce the bias in learning from imbalanced data sets. Different from previous review articles on imbalanced learning that almost exclusively focus on binary classification in the classical machine learning context, in this paper, we also discuss the more recent challenges such as imbalanced regression, unsupervised learning like clustering and the deep long-tailed learning.

Present research on addressing the problem of class imbalance has proliferated in the last decade and existing works are generally grouped into five categories, namely data level preprocessing, algorithm level improvement, hybrid schemes, ensemble methods and techniques for handling data streams. In this paper, we present a more up-to-date survey of the literature by exploring in more detail the various application domains of imbalance learning such as bioinformatics, network security, industrial fault diagnosis and business intelligence. The survey finally concludes with six open research issues and recommends several important research trajectories for the next generation of imbalance learning researchers, such as multimodal imbalanced learning and design of distributed algorithms based on the concept of federated learning under class skew.
